topic: canopy airflow and fungal disease risk

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Dense foliage around grape bunches restricts airflow and retains humidity after rain or dew.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Opening the canopy through shoot positioning or leaf removal improves ventilation and speeds the drying of bunches.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Conditions become less favourable for Botrytis and other fungal diseases, while an excessively dense canopy increases disease pressure.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: Conditions become less favourable for Botrytis and other fungal diseases, while an excessively dense canopy increases disease pressure.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
